name: Trail to El Chorrerón
description: Hike the moderate 10.7-mile Trail to El Chorrerón in Paraje Natural El Chorrerón, leading to a stunning 39-meter waterfall.
region_intro: Hiking around Moraleja offers diverse terrain with accessible paths suitable for various fitness levels. The region features a mix of riverine landscapes, open countryside, and areas with moderate elevation changes. Trails often follow natural watercourses like the Rivera de Gata Canal, providing varied scenery. The local environment supports both leisurely strolls and more demanding routes. Best hiking trails around Moraleja The most popular hiking route is Trail to El Chorrerón, a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) moderate trail that takes about 4 hours 22 minutes to complete, featuring gentle elevation changes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Medieval Bridge of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria, an easy 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This route explores historical landmarks and river views. Local hikers also love the Mirador del Boquerón loop from Cachorrilla, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) moderate trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Moraleja is defined by accessible paths, river valleys, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate routes with some elevation gain. The routes in Moraleja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore Moraleja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Moraleja?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes around Moraleja, offering a variety of experiences. These include 6 easy trails and 5 moderate trails, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Moraleja?
A: Hiking around Moraleja features diverse terrain, including accessible paths, riverine landscapes, and open countryside. You'll find routes that follow natural watercourses like the Rivera de Gata Canal, with gentle to moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Moraleja?
A: Yes, Moraleja offers several easy routes suitable for families. An excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31888302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.6-mile (7.4 km) path that explores historical landmarks and river views, perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to see on the trails?
A: Many trails offer scenic views and natural features. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38141672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Boquerón loop from Cachorrilla&lt;/a&gt; leads through varied landscapes and offers a viewpoint. You can also explore areas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5995492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path to the Mirador del Boquerón and Laguna de los Jornus&lt;/a&gt;, which provides beautiful natural scenery.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in the Moraleja region?
A: Yes, many routes around Moraleja are designed as circular walks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38141672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Boquerón loop from Cachorrilla&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31890929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Gata Canal – El Chorrerón Natural Area loop from Moraleja&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic circular experience.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31888302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria – Coria Castle and Cathedral loop from Coria&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5690813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villasbuenas de Gata Village Square&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish of Our Lady of the Good Man, Hoyos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Moraleja?
A: The hiking trails around Moraleja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Hikers often praise the accessible paths, varied landscapes, and the peaceful river valleys that define the region's routes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like lakes or rivers?
A: Many trails in Moraleja follow natural watercourses. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31890929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivera de Gata Canal – El Chorrerón Natural Area loop from Moraleja&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering views along the canal. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3333558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borbollón Dam&lt;/a&gt; and natural swimming spots such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5992396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piscina Natural La Alameda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Moraleja?
A: Moraleja offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Even in winter, many paths remain accessible, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While Moraleja primarily offers easy to moderate trails, some routes provide a good challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276749508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to El Chorrerón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.7-mile (17.2 km) route that takes over four hours, offering a longer excursion. For more significant elevation, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31428843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyos – Historic Center of Hoyos loop from Hoyos&lt;/a&gt; has the most elevation gain among the listed routes.

Q: What are the typical durations for hikes in Moraleja?
A: Hikes in Moraleja vary in duration. Easy trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31888302" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria loop&lt;/a&gt;, can be completed in under two hours. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276749508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to El Chorrerón&lt;/a&gt;, can take around four hours or more, depending on your pace.
tips: Moraleja is a Spanish municipality, located in the province of Cáceres, autonomous community of Extremadura. It includes the town of the same name and an extensive municipal area in which there are several small hamlets and colonization towns such as Cañadas and Rozacorderos.
This is a very interesting site. There is a gorge with a waterfall to a very beautiful natural pool. Also, there is a wooden and stone bridge to cross the river.

faqs.question: How much time should I allocate for the hike to El Chorrerón?
faqs.answer: The full circular route, known as the Trail to El Chorrerón, typically takes around 4 hours and 25 minutes to complete. While the downhill section to the waterfall can be done in under 30 minutes at a relaxed pace, the return journey is a challenging uphill climb, requiring more time and effort.

faqs.question: What is the best way to reach the trailhead for El Chorrerón?
faqs.answer: The trailhead for the Trail to El Chorrerón is best accessed with an SUV or a 4x4 vehicle due to the mountainous terrain. There is parking available on private land near the entrance to the waterfall area.

faqs.question: Is the Trail to El Chorrerón suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is categorized as moderately difficult. While it offers a rewarding experience, the significant uphill climb on the return journey from the waterfall makes it challenging, especially for beginners. Hikers should be prepared for steep sections and uneven terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Trail to El Chorrerón?
faqs.answer: The terrain is largely untouched and wild, featuring a steep path that demands careful footing. It begins with a short stepped section and a handrail, but quickly transitions to a natural dirt and stone path. You'll traverse pine- and oak-covered mountains.

faqs.question: What can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main attraction is the dramatic 39-meter El Chorrerón Waterfall, which plunges into the Pichigua River. You'll also pass through serene mountain villages and enjoy views of pine- and oak-covered landscapes. The route also includes the Parish Church of the Assumption of Moraleja.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit El Chorrerón?
faqs.answer: The area enjoys a cool climate year-round due to its mountainous location. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, visiting during drier months might offer better trail conditions, though the waterfall's flow might be less dramatic than during the rainy season.

faqs.question: Are there any entrance fees or permits required for the Trail to El Chorrerón?
faqs.answer: Yes, access to the El Chorrerón Waterfall area is on private land and requires a small entrance fee, which is paid directly to caretakers on site. No specific permits are mentioned beyond this fee.

faqs.question: Is the Trail to El Chorrerón dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether dogs are allowed on the trail or within the private land area of El Chorrerón Waterfall. It's advisable to check with local caretakers before bringing pets.

faqs.question: Are there amenities available at El Chorrerón Waterfall?
faqs.answer: Basic amenities are available, including rustic restrooms and picnic tables. However, there are no nearby restaurants, so visitors are encouraged to bring their own food and water.

faqs.question: Can I swim at El Chorrerón Waterfall?
faqs.answer: Yes, swimming is possible in the deep, five-meter pool at the base of the waterfall. However, be aware that the current near the falls can be strong.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for the hike?
faqs.answer: Hikers are strongly advised to wear closed-toe shoes with good traction due to the steep and uneven terrain. Trekking poles are highly recommended to alleviate knee strain, especially during the uphill ascent. Bring plenty of water and your own food, as there are no restaurants nearby.
